title: Evidence-Gated Push Infrastructure Cartography
author: cartographer
mission: Map existing checkpoint, publication, prepublication review, git integration, lane binder, claim tracking, and dirty file detection infrastructure to design a push system where push feels earned, not merely possible.

gates_today: Checkpoint records created with sessionID, messageID, title, failure report
Persisted to Storage service under ["checkpoint", sessionID, id] key
Published flag tracked in CheckpointRecord type (published: boolean)
gates_missing: No git commit created — checkpoints are session-level artifacts only
No SHA binding between checkpoint and git HEAD at time of creation
Two parallel systems exist: Effect-based (checkpoint.ts) and plugin-based (prepare_checkpoint.ts)
No validation that workspace is clean before checkpoint
No link from checkpoint to publication record
data_needed: Git HEAD SHA at checkpoint time
Workspace dirty file list at checkpoint time
List of lanes whose binders are closed
systems_mapped.needs_change: Consolidate dual systems. Add git SHA capture at checkpoint creation. Add workspace-clean pre-condition. Wire checkpoint SHA into publication record chain of custody.

gates_today: Publication record structure exists with 22+ fields: identity, claim_atoms, evidence, alternatives rejected, boundary, capabilities, tests, review cycle
Publication ledger exists as append-only JSONL at publications.v1.jsonl
Tracks checkpoint_sha, branch, boundary, claim_atoms, changed_files, deferred_seams, prepublication_verdict, publisher_session, published_at
gates_missing: NO SCHEMA FILE EXISTS at docs/schemas/rig.relay.builder_publication_record.v1.schema.json

v1→v2 field drift: v2 drops 8+ fields including candidates_packet_digest, approval_artifact, alternatives_rejected, intended_released_boundary, schemas_and_audits_produced, external_effects, known_dirty_paths, proposed_reviewer_attack_surface, publication_authorization
No JSON Schema validation — records are written with JSON.stringify() directly
No validation script exists (scripts/rig_relay_validate_schemas.py is absent)
No machine-verifiable link between publication record and actual git push
systems_mapped.needs_change: Create the schema file. Consolidate v1/v2 field set. Add push binding fields. Wire schema validation into publication tooling.

gates_today: One QA verification artifact exists at docs/json/qa/ with per-claim PASS/FAIL scoring
Covers typecheck verification per package and test results
AGENTS.md mandates a verification_record schema and workflow
gates_missing: NO VERIFICATION RECORD SCHEMA EXISTS at docs/schemas/rig.relay.verification_record.v1.schema.json
The existing QA artifact uses schema_version "rig.relay.qa.verification.v1" — also no schema file
No standardized verification format — the single existing artifact is ad-hoc
No machine-readable gates from verification into publication flow
systems_mapped.needs_change: Create verification record schema. Standardize per-claim verification format. Wire verification status into push gate evaluation.

gates_today: Three dedicated tools for recording prepublication verdicts (admitted, blocked, inconclusive)
Prepublication conductor dispatches 7 specialist adversaries for review
Verdict log recorded at prepublication_verdicts.v1.jsonl (5 entries, 3 review rounds)
Per-verdict artifacts written to docs/json/opencode/approvals/
Review cycle tracks rounds, blocking findings, repairs applied, final disposition
Publisher agent requires prepublication_admitted before push
gates_missing: NO PREPUBLICATION REVIEW CYCLE SCHEMA at docs/schemas/rig.relay.prepublication_review_cycle.v1.schema.json
No machine-verifiable SHA binding between verdict and candidate boundary
Two parallel record systems: individual files (per-verdict) AND aggregated JSONL — no cross-validation
No automatic gate: verdicts are advisory, not enforced programmatically
No expiry/ staleness detection on prepublication verdicts
data_needed: SHA256 candidate packet digest → verdict binding
Freshness check: verdict issued_at must predate any subsequent edits
Waiver tracking for red-team findings deemed acceptable
systems_mapped.needs_change: Create prepublication review cycle schema. Add SHA binding between verdict and code state. Add programmatic gate enforcement. Add freshness/ staleness detection.

gates_today: smart_git supports 12 git operations with some gating
Push: --force/-f/--delete blocked at code level
Stash: drop/clear blocked; Checkout: -- HEAD~ blocked; Branch: -D/--delete blocked
Bash rerouting captures git commands and redirects to smart_git
Historian agent with 6 parallel subagents (blame-tracer, diff-composer, message-writer, pr-crafter, review-responder, release-note-writer)
Publisher agent is the ONLY agent with git push permission
Publisher requires: prepublication_admitted, matching digest, matching SHA, chronology check, named authorization
gates_missing: git commit --amend blocked by policy only, not code (smart_git lacks --amend guard)
git rebase/git merge not supported by smart_git but not blocked by bash rerouting either
No pre-push hook or gate list — publisher's checks are procedural, not programmatic
No SHA binding between checkpoint, review cycle, and pushed commit
No dirty-file check at push time
No lane-binder closure check at push time
No verification record check at push time
smart_git commit has no validation of message format, no signing enforcement
data_needed: SHA chain of custody: checkpoint SHA → review round SHA → pushed SHA
systems_mapped.needs_change: Add programmatic push gate enforcement in smart_git/publisher. Add SHA chain-of-custody verification. Add dirty-file gate. Add lane-binder gate. Add verification gate.

gates_today: Lane lifecycle defined: idle → cartography → planning → reviewing → executing → validating → repairing → completed/failed/blocked
Secretary tracks lane state machine with pure reducer
BinderService has 22-field interface including: evidence chain, execution events, validation results, red-team findings, repair history, checkpoint commit, residual risks
Wave tracking tools record execution and stress waves to JSONL
Handoff messages encode lane_id, status, waves_completed, claims_verified, blockers
gates_missing: BINDER PERSISTENCE IS STUBBED: getBinders() returns [] — no binders survive past in-memory lifetime
Secretary's Binder and BinderService.Binder are different types — not synchronized
No programmatic gate evaluating binder closure before push
claim_atoms verification is manual (validator agent reads code) — no automated attestation
No machine-readable 'all lanes closed' aggregate state
Lane dependencies tracked in metadata but not enforced programmatically
data_needed: Persistent binder storage (file or DB-backed, not in-memory)
systems_mapped.needs_change: Implement persistent binder storage. Synchronize Secretary and BinderService. Add programmatic lane closure evaluation. Wire binder digest into push gate.

gates_today: Claim_atoms flow: architect proposes → plan artifact stores → critic reviews → executor receives → validator checks → lane-owner reports verified count
claim_atoms must be non-empty JSON array (enforced at propose_plan time)
Claim-adversary subagent attacks claims with 5 outcome types: falsified_blocking, survived_attack, unproven_material, deferred_outside_boundary, informational
gates_missing: No automated claim verification against actual code — validator reads manually
No evidence SHA binding — claims reference files but no digest verification
No aggregate claim status across all lanes for push gating
completion_pct tracked in roadmap_progress but not machine-gated
No per-claim attestation artifact that survives beyond session lifetime
data_needed: Automated claim verification tool or test
Aggregate claim dashboard across all lanes
Claim attestation artifacts with machine-readable PASS/FAIL
systems_mapped.needs_change: Add automated claim verification capability. Add SHA evidence binding. Add aggregate claim status computation. Make claim attestation a push gate input.

gates_today: Operating Picture Service reads git status → dirtyFiles list
smart_git status returns structured file list (path, staged, unstaged, untracked counts)
AGENTS.md policy: dirty files are PROTECTED, must preserve parallel lane changes
smart_write captures post-write diff for verification
gates_missing: NO DIRTY FILE GATE exists in any push or publication path
Publisher.md has no dirty file precondition — can push with dirty files
No 'unprotected files' concept — all dirty files treated equally
No diff SHA capture for dirty files at checkpoint time
No mechanism to attest 'workspace was clean at push time'
Preflight_check tool exists but has no dirty file check logic
data_needed: Workspace cleanliness attestation (SHA of git status output)
Unprotected vs protected dirty file distinction
Dirty file cert in publication record
systems_mapped.needs_change: Add dirty file gate to publisher/push path. Add 'unprotected' file concept. Add workspace-clean attestation to publication record. Add preflight_check for dirty files.

cross_cutting_gaps.summary: Three mandated JSON Schemas do not exist
cross_cutting_gaps.detail: AGENTS.md mandates builder_publication_record.v1.schema.json, verification_record.v1.schema.json, and prepublication_review_cycle.v1.schema.json. None exist. The validate script (rig_relay_validate_schemas.py) is also absent.

cross_cutting_gaps.summary: SHA chain of custody is missing
cross_cutting_gaps.detail: There is no machine-verifiable binding between checkpoint creation, review cycle verdicts, and pushed commit. Publisher checks rely on agent reading artifacts, not hash verification.

Publisher.md lists 5 preconditions but they are procedural (agent reads artifacts). No tool evaluates 'can push now' by checking: all lane binders closed, dirty files clean, prepublication admitted, verification passed, red-team findings waived.

cross_cutting_gaps.summary: Dual checkpoint systems with no consolidation
cross_cutting_gaps.detail: Effect-based checkpoint.ts (Storage service) and plugin-based prepare_checkpoint.ts (JSONL) exist in parallel with different schemas and no cross-linking.
BinderService.getBinders() returns empty array. No binders survive past session lifetime. The Integrator.mergeLaneCheckpoints will fail with NoBindersError.
v2 drops 8+ fields from v1 without schema governance. No single authoritative field set for builder_publication_record.
A prepublication_admitted verdict from 3 days ago would still satisfy the publisher check even if code has changed significantly since.

cross_cutting_gaps.summary: No aggregate lane state for push gating
cross_cutting_gaps.detail: Each lane tracks its own state. There is no 'all lanes closed and healthy' aggregate computation that could serve as a push gate input.

cross_cutting_gaps.summary: Claim verification is manual, not automated
cross_cutting_gaps.detail: Validator agent reads code to verify claim_atoms. No test-level or schema-level automated verification exists.
Blocked only by AGENTS.md policy, not code. smart_git commit only supports -m but no --amend guard. Could be exploited via bash fallthrough.

cross_cutting_gaps.summary: Pre-existing findings relevant to this work
cross_cutting_gaps.detail: Eight pre-existing debt findings exist from prior sessions including never-called functions, misnamed variables, placeholder tools, and cross-reference gaps. These are not blockers but indicate general schema/tool discipline gaps.

proposed_gates.current_status: NOT IMPLEMENTED — binders are in-memory only

proposed_gates.source: prepublication_verdicts.v1.jsonl → latest round = admitted, SHA matches HEAD
proposed_gates.current_status: PARTIAL — tooling exists but SHA binding missing
NOT IMPLEMENTED — no schema, no gate

proposed_gates.gate: All red-team findings closed or waived
proposed_gates.source: stress wave records → no falsified_blocking; waivers recorded
proposed_gates.current_status: PARTIAL — stress wave recording exists but no aggregate waiver tracking
NOT IMPLEMENTED — no audit gate exists

proposed_gates.source: git status → empty (or only known-protected files dirty)
proposed_gates.current_status: NOT IMPLEMENTED — no dirty file gate in publisher
NOT IMPLEMENTED — no automated claim verification

proposed_gates.source: checkpoint SHA → review SHA → pushed SHA chain intact

gate_evaluator.needed_tool: push_gate_check tool that evaluates all 8 gates atomically
gate_evaluator.output: Structured gate manifest passed to publisher agent

architecture_recommendations.rationale: No schema governance = no machine-readable gates = push can't be earned

architecture_recommendations.detail: Move BinderService from in-memory Ref<Map> to file or database-backed persistence
architecture_recommendations.rationale: Without persisted binders, lane closure is ephemeral and unrecoverable

architecture_recommendations.action: Add SHA chain-of-custody to checkpoint system
architecture_recommendations.detail: Capture git HEAD SHA at checkpoint creation; bind into CheckpointRecord and propagate through review cycle to publication record
architecture_recommendations.rationale: Without SHA binding, prepublication admission can't be linked to the code it reviewed

architecture_recommendations.detail: Evaluate all 8 proposed gates atomically, produce structured gate manifest
architecture_recommendations.rationale: Without programmatic gate evaluation, 'push feels earned' is aspirational

architecture_recommendations.action: Add dirty-file gate to publisher path
architecture_recommendations.detail: Preflight check for dirty files before push; require clean workspace or explicit waiver
architecture_recommendations.rationale: Pushing with dirty files breaks the audit trail

architecture_recommendations.detail: Unify Effect-based and plugin-based checkpoint implementations under one schema
architecture_recommendations.rationale: Two parallel systems with drift risk erode trust in checkpoint records

architecture_recommendations.action: Add freshness/ staleness detection to prepublication verdicts
architecture_recommendations.detail: Detect if code has changed since prepublication admission; invalidate stale verdicts
Reconcile v1/v2 field drift into single authoritative set matching the new schema
Allow claim_atoms to be verified by test or tool rather than only manual agent inspection
architecture_recommendations.rationale: Manual claim verification is slow, expensive, and not machine-gateable

pre_existing_findings_relevant.summary: Two tools in registry are placeholders (delegate.ts, session-diff.ts)
pre_existing_findings_relevant.relevance: Erodes trust in tool registry — evidence-gated push depends on tool integrity

pre_existing_findings_relevant.summary: sanitizedProcessEnv() renamed from no-op to actual filtering
pre_existing_findings_relevant.relevance: Example of function-level drift that schema governance would catch
